message:
✨ Detect friendly cluster name when running in-cluster

When running inside a Kubernetes cluster, the console previously showed
"in-cluster" as the cluster name in the GPU reservations dropdown and
other UIs. This was confusing for users.

Now on OpenShift, the console reads the Infrastructure/cluster resource
to extract the API server URL and derives a friendly name from it
(e.g. "fmaas-vllm-d" from api.fmaas-vllm-d.fmaas.res.ibm.com).
Falls back to "in-cluster" on non-OpenShift or if detection fails.

Both GetClient and GetDynamicClient accept the detected name as an
alias for the in-cluster context.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
